description: Fix PID file JSON parsing bug in CLI status and doctor commands — both use parseInt() on JSON content, which always returns NaN, breaking PID display and stale PID cleanup.

userStories.title: Fix PID file JSON parsing in status and doctor commands
userStories.description: As a user running `opentabs status` or `opentabs doctor` after starting the server with `--background`, I want the PID and background indicator to display correctly so that I can see the server's process ID and clean up stale PID files.
acceptanceCriteria: Both `status.ts` and `doctor.ts` parse the PID file using JSON-aware parsing that handles the `{"pid":N,"port":P}` format written by `start.ts`
When a background server is running, `opentabs status` shows the PID line with '(background)' indicator
When a background server is running, `opentabs doctor` shows '(background, PID N)' in the server health detail
Stale PID file cleanup works: when the PID file references a dead process, both commands delete the stale PID file
Backward compatibility: plain-integer PID files (legacy format) are still parsed correctly
userStories.notes:
Root cause: `start.ts` line 383 writes the PID file as JSON: `JSON.stringify({ pid, port })`, producing content like `{"pid":1234,"port":8888}`. But `status.ts` line 148 and `doctor.ts` line 472 both parse it with `parseInt(await readFile(pidPath, 'utf-8'), 10)`. `parseInt('{"pid":1234}', 10)` returns NaN because the string starts with `{`. The `!isNaN(pid)` check then fails, silently skipping both the PID display and the stale PID cleanup code.

The correct parser already exists in `stop.ts` lines 62-78 as `parsePidFile()`. It handles both JSON and plain-integer legacy formats. The fix is to extract `parsePidFile` to a shared location (e.g., `platform/cli/src/config.ts` which already exports `getPidFilePath`) and use it in all three files.

Files to modify:
- `platform/cli/src/commands/status.ts` — line 148: replace `parseInt(...)` with JSON-aware parsing
- `platform/cli/src/commands/doctor.ts` — line 472: same fix
- `platform/cli/src/commands/stop.ts` — lines 62-78: extract `parsePidFile` to shared location, import it back
- `platform/cli/src/config.ts` — add the shared `parsePidFile` export

Do NOT change the PID file write format in start.ts — it is correct. Only fix the readers.

The `parsePidFile` function from stop.ts handles both formats:
1. Try JSON.parse — extract `.pid` and `.port` from the object
2. Fall back to parseInt for plain-integer legacy format
3. Return null if neither works
